fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Vermont Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Vermont - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity vermont, how to transfer audiologist license to vermont, audiologist compact vermont, vermont audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Vermont Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Vermont Secretary of State, Office of Professional Regulation
board.mailing_address: Office of Professional Regulation, 89 Main Street, 3rd Floor, Montpelier, VT 05620-3402

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Vermont.

steps.title: Create an Account with Vermont OPR Online Services
steps.description: Navigate to the Vermont OPR Online Services portal (secure.vermont.gov/opl) and select 'Register' to establish a new user profile if you do not already have one.

steps.title: Initiate the Audiologist Endorsement Application
steps.description: Log in to your Vermont OPR Online Services account, click 'Apply for a License', and select the 'Audiologist by Endorsement' application type.

steps.title: Complete Application and Pay Fee Online
steps.description: Fill in all required information on the application form, upload any necessary personal documents (such as background information or attestations), and submit the $150 application fee through the portal.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your educational institution and verification of licensure from all states where you currently or previously held an audiologist license to be sent directly to the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation.

steps.title: Await Review and Licensing Decision
steps.description: The Office of Professional Regulation (OPR) will process your complete application, which includes review by the Board of Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists. You will be notified of any deficiencies or the final licensing decision via the portal or email.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Vermont audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Vermont have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Vermont participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Vermont without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Vermont?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Vermont while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Vermont
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Vermont by endorsement, register and submit your application through the Vermont OPR Online Services portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the Vermont OPR Online Services portal (secure.vermont.gov/opl), click 'Register' for new users, enter your email and create a password, confirm your email, then log in to start an application.
